canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, SummEval is consistently characterized as a benchmark dataset used to evaluate text summarization. Its primary stated purpose is to assess text summary quality, which one paper frames more broadly as the evaluation of text in terms of "high-level concepts." The operationalization of this measurement relies on human evaluation, with one source specifying that annotations are provided by three human raters. A prevalent framing across multiple papers is that these human judgments are structured along four specific dimensions: fluency, coherence, consistency, and relevance. This evaluation is captured using Likert-scale scores, which one paper details as ranging from 1 to 5. In line with this design, the dataset is explicitly used to perform "text summarization evaluation."
papers: CanLLMs Ground when they (Don’t) Know: A Study on Direct and Loaded Political Questions
ComparingLLM-generated and human-authored news text using formal syntactic theory
Inferring from Logits: Exploring Best Practices for Decoding-Free Generative Candidate Selection
Internal Value Alignment in Large Language Models through Controlled Value Vector Activation
VLMInferSlow: Evaluating the Efficiency Robustness of Large Vision-Language Models as a Service
Interdisciplinary Research in Conversation: A Case Study in Computational Morphology for Language Documentation
Task-Aware Resolution Optimization for Visual Large Language Models
iVISPAR— An Interactive Visual-Spatial Reasoning Benchmark forVLMs
Beyond correlation: The impact of human uncertainty in measuring the effectiveness of automatic evaluation and LLM-as-a-judge
MetaMetrics: Calibrating Metrics for Generation Tasks Using Human Preferences
RevisEval: Improving LLM-as-a-Judge via Response-Adapted References

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset for evaluating text summary quality across four criteria (fluency, coherence, consistency, and relevance) using human-assigned Likert scores from 1 to 5.
definitions.paper: Beyond correlation: The impact of human uncertainty in measuring the effectiveness of automatic evaluation and LLM-as-a-judge

relationships.paper: MetaMetrics: Calibrating Metrics for Generation Tasks Using Human Preferences
relationships.evidence: For this task, we use the SummEval (Fabbri et al., 2021) for text summarization evaluation. (Section 4)
